title: limonium oleifolium subsp. sardoum
intro: Limonium oleifolium subsp. sardoum, also known as Sardinian sea lavender, is a plant species belonging to the family Plumbaginaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Sardinian sea lavender include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and ointments, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
